CKV_K8S_1.description: Using the 'latest' tag for container images can lead to unpredictable behavior and potential security risks. It is recommended to specify a specific version or tag for container images.

CKV_K8S_2.description: Running containers in privileged mode can pose security risks. It is recommended to avoid using privileged containers unless absolutely necessary.

CKV_K8S_3.description: Running containers as root can pose security risks. It is recommended to run containers with a non-root user whenever possible.

CKV_K8S_4.description: Enabling host networking can pose security risks. It is recommended to avoid using host networking unless absolutely necessary.

CKV_K8S_5.description: Host PID namespace can pose security risks. It is recommended to avoid using host PID namespace unless absolutely necessary.

CKV_K8S_6.description: Host IPC namespace can pose security risks. It is recommended to avoid using host IPC namespace unless absolutely necessary.

CKV_K8S_7.description: AllowPrivilegeEscalation can pose security risks. It is recommended to set AllowPrivilegeEscalation to false unless absolutely necessary.

CKV_K8S_8.description: HostPath volumes allow containers to mount files or directories directly from the host node filesystem. This can expose sensitive host resources and increase the risk of container escape or node compromise.

CKV_K8S_9.description: automountServiceAccountToken enabled in Kubernetes allows Kubernetes to automatically mount service account credentials inside pods. If a container is compromised, attackers may use the exposed token to access the Kubernetes API and perform unauthorized actions based on the assigned RBAC permissions

CKV_K8S_10.description: Running containers with a read-only root filesystem can enhance security by preventing unauthorized modifications to the container's filesystem. It is recommended to set the root filesystem to read-only unless the application requires write access.

CKV_K8S_11.description: Containers without CPU limits can consume excessive CPU resources, potentially affecting cluster stability and other workloads. It is recommended to define CPU limits for all containers.

CKV_K8S_12.description: Containers without memory limits can consume excessive memory resources, potentially causing node instability or OOM (Out Of Memory) issues. It is recommended to define memory limits for all containers.
